title: Retail-trading Apps Go Upscale to Keep Rich Traders Loyal
summary: Robinhood, eToro and Public are racing to keep their young clientele from “graduating” to Wall Street wealth managers with gala events, exclusive concierges, and platinum cards. Charlie Wells has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Forint Surges as Orban’s Loss Starts New Era for Hungary-EU Ties
summary: Hungary’s forint surged after Prime Minister Viktor Orban conceded defeat in Sunday’s election, with the pro-European opposition’s landslide victory expected to help unlock billions of euros in European Union funding.

title: Wary Investors Eye Another Escalation in Iran War
summary: The prospect that the Iran war will reintensify after the failure of peace talks threatens to spark fresh volatility across global markets, after a week that saw a fragile ceasefire drive stocks up and oil down by the most this year.

title: Geopolitical Shock Tests Moat Strategies As Energy Surges
Are There Still Opportunities In Europe And Asia Despite High Oil Prices?
Oil Shock, AI Tailwinds, And Portfolio Shifts Across Emerging Markets
Wall Street Brunch: Earnings Arrive Amid Hormuz Standoff
Trump Says US to Sever Iran Lifeline With Blockade of Strait
summary: President Donald Trump said the US will blockade the Strait of Hormuz following the failure of peace talks with Iran in Islamabad this weekend, a move that will likely exacerbate oil and fuel shortages globally.

title: Peru Extends Voting for President, Congress Amid Delays at Polling Sites
summary: Peru’s nationwide elections suffered severe delays at some polling stations, especially in the capital Lima, muddying what was already a complex race among a record 36 presidential candidates.
